name: Albert Einstein
description: Physicist Albert Einstein — life, theories, and legacy

questions.q: In which country was Albert Einstein born in 1879?

source.notes: Born in Ulm, in the Kingdom of Württemberg, German Empire.

questions.q: What is Albert Einstein's most famous equation?

source.notes: Published in 1905; expresses mass-energy equivalence.

questions.q: Which musical instrument did Einstein play from childhood and continue throughout his life?

source.notes: Said his greatest joy in life was music.

questions.q: What religious or cultural heritage was Einstein born into?

source.notes: Non-observant Ashkenazi Jewish family.

questions.q: Einstein spent the last decades of his life working at Princeton University in which U.S. state?

source.notes: At the Institute for Advanced Study, 1933–1955.

questions.q: In what year did Albert Einstein die?

source.notes: Died April 18, 1955 in Princeton, NJ, at age 76.

questions.q: Einstein famously renounced the citizenship of which country in the 1930s?

source.notes: Renounced German citizenship in 1933 after the Nazis took power.

questions.q: Einstein was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics for his work on what phenomenon?

source.notes: 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics (awarded 1922).

questions.q: Einstein's sweeping theory of gravity, published in 1915, is known as his theory of what?

source.notes: Published in 1915; extended special relativity to gravity.

questions.q: In which Swiss capital did Einstein work at the Patent Office from 1903 to 1909?

source.notes: Worked as a Technical Assistant examiner while developing his revolutionary 1905 papers.

questions.q: In what year did Einstein acquire Swiss citizenship?

source.notes: Retained Swiss citizenship his entire life.

questions.q: Einstein emigrated to the United States in which year, fleeing the rise of Nazi Germany?

source.notes: Never returned to Germany.

questions.q: Time Magazine named Einstein its 'Person of the' what at the end of the 1990s?

source.notes: Named 'Person of the Century' in December 1999.

questions.q: After separating from his first wife, Mileva, in 1914, Einstein moved to which German city?

source.notes: Took a professorship at the University of Berlin and led the Kaiser Wilhelm Institute.

questions.q: The Einstein family moved in 1880 to which German city, where young Albert spent much of his childhood?

source.notes: Family moved from Ulm when Albert was about a year old.

questions.q: In his 1939 letter to which U.S. President did Einstein warn about the prospect of German atomic weapons?

source.notes: Letter co-signed with physicist Leo Szilard; helped spark the Manhattan Project.

questions.q: What toy is said to have sparked Einstein's interest in science at age 5?

source.notes: Einstein recalled being fascinated by the invisible force moving the needle.

questions.q: What was the first name of Einstein's first wife, a fellow student in physics at Zurich Polytechnic?

source.notes: Serbian physicist Mileva Marić; married 1903, divorced 1919.

questions.q: In what German town was Albert Einstein born?

source.notes: Born 14 March 1879 in Ulm, Kingdom of Württemberg.

questions.q: In 1952, Einstein was offered the presidency of which country, an offer he declined?

source.notes: Offered after Chaim Weizmann's death; Einstein politely declined.

questions.q: Einstein's 'miracle year' of 1905 saw him publish how many groundbreaking papers?

source.notes: The Annus Mirabilis papers: photoelectric effect, Brownian motion, special relativity, and mass-energy equivalence.

questions.q: According to Einstein's divorce agreement with Mileva, he promised her the money from what, should he ever win it?

source.notes: Mileva received the prize money when Einstein won the 1921 Nobel.
